title: 51. Who is known as the Father of the Indian Constitution?
hint: He was the Chairman of the Drafting Committee.
summary: Dr. B.R. Ambedkar played the leading role in drafting the Constitution of India.

title: 52. How many Fundamental Rights are currently guaranteed by the Constitution of India?
hint: Originally there were seven; one was later removed.
summary: At present, there are six Fundamental Rights in the Constitution.

title: 53. What is the supreme law of India?
hint: All laws must conform to it.
summary: The Constitution is the highest legal authority in India.

title: 54. Who is the constitutional head of the Union Government?
hint: This office is elected indirectly.
summary: The President is the constitutional head of India.

title: 55. Which part of the Constitution contains Fundamental Rights?
hint: It comes immediately before the Directive Principles.
summary: Part III of the Constitution contains Fundamental Rights.

title: 56. Which Article abolishes untouchability?
hint: It is one of the shortest but most important Fundamental Rights articles.
summary: Article 17 abolishes untouchability and forbids its practice.

title: 57. Who appoints the Prime Minister of India?
hint: The appointment is formal, but based on majority support in Lok Sabha.
summary: The President appoints the Prime Minister.

title: 58. Which house of Parliament is known as the Upper House?
hint: Its members are not directly elected by all citizens.
summary: Rajya Sabha is the Upper House of Parliament.

title: 59. What is the minimum voting age in India?
hint: It was reduced by the 61st Constitutional Amendment.
summary: Indian citizens can vote after attaining 18 years of age.

title: 60. Which body conducts elections in India?
hint: It is an independent constitutional body.
summary: The Election Commission of India conducts elections.

title: 61. Which Article guarantees the Right to Constitutional Remedies?
hint: Dr. Ambedkar called it the heart and soul of the Constitution.
summary: Article 32 allows citizens to approach the Supreme Court for enforcement of Fundamental Rights.

title: 62. Which constitutional amendment added Fundamental Duties?
hint: This amendment is often called the Mini Constitution.
summary: The 42nd Constitutional Amendment Act, 1976 added Fundamental Duties.

title: 63. Which Schedule deals with the Anti-Defection Law?
hint: It was inserted by the 52nd Amendment.
summary: The Tenth Schedule contains provisions related to defection.

title: 64. Who presides over a joint sitting of Parliament?
hint: The officer belongs to the directly elected House.
summary: The Speaker of Lok Sabha presides over a joint sitting.

title: 65. Which Article deals with the proclamation of National Emergency?
hint: This Article is frequently asked in UPSC prelims.
summary: Article 352 provides for National Emergency.

title: 66. Which Constitutional Amendment lowered the voting age from 21 years to 18 years?
hint: This amendment was passed in 1988 and expanded democratic participation.
summary: The 61st Constitutional Amendment Act, 1988 reduced the voting age from 21 to 18 years.

title: 67. Who is the ex-officio Chairman of the Rajya Sabha?
hint: This person is also the second-highest constitutional authority in India.
summary: The Vice President of India serves as the ex-officio Chairman of the Rajya Sabha.

title: 68. Which Article provides for the establishment of the Finance Commission?
hint: This constitutional body is constituted every five years.
summary: Article 280 provides for the Finance Commission.

title: 69. Which Constitutional Amendment gave constitutional status to Panchayati Raj Institutions?
hint: It was enacted in 1992 and relates to rural local self-government.
summary: The 73rd Constitutional Amendment Act provided constitutional status to Panchayati Raj Institutions.

title: 70. What is the maximum strength of the Lok Sabha as provided by the Constitution?
hint: The actual current strength is lower than this constitutional limit.
summary: The Constitution provides for a maximum strength of 552 members in the Lok Sabha.

title: 71. Which writ is issued to produce a detained person before a court?
hint: Its Latin meaning is 'you may have the body'.
summary: Habeas Corpus safeguards personal liberty against unlawful detention.

title: 72. Which feature of the Indian Constitution was borrowed from the Constitution of Ireland?
hint: These principles guide the State in governance but are not enforceable in court.
summary: Directive Principles of State Policy were borrowed from the Irish Constitution.

title: 73. Which body is known as the guardian of the Constitution?
hint: It has the power of judicial review.
summary: The Supreme Court protects and interprets the Constitution.

title: 74. Which Constitutional Amendment granted constitutional status to Urban Local Bodies?
hint: This amendment is often studied together with the 73rd Amendment.
summary: The 74th Constitutional Amendment Act provided constitutional status to Municipalities.

title: 75. Which Article empowers the President to promulgate Ordinances?
hint: This power can be used when Parliament is not in session.
summary: Article 123 empowers the President to promulgate Ordinances.

title: 76. Which Schedule of the Constitution contains the Union, State and Concurrent Lists?
hint: This schedule is fundamental to India's federal structure.
summary: The Seventh Schedule distributes legislative powers between the Union and States.

title: 77. In which case did the Supreme Court propound the Basic Structure Doctrine?
hint: The case involved Parliament's power to amend the Constitution.
summary: The Kesavananda Bharati case (1973) established the Basic Structure Doctrine.

title: 78. Which Constitutional Amendment inserted the words 'Socialist' and 'Secular' into the Preamble?
hint: The same amendment also added Fundamental Duties.
summary: The 42nd Constitutional Amendment inserted the words Socialist and Secular into the Preamble.

title: 79. Which emergency has never been proclaimed in India till date?
hint: It is related to the country's financial stability.
summary: Financial Emergency under Article 360 has never been imposed in India.

title: 80. Which constitutional body audits the accounts of the Union and State Governments?
hint: This authority is often called the guardian of the public purse.
summary: The Comptroller and Auditor General (CAG) audits government accounts.

title: 81. Which Article of the Constitution provides for President's Rule in a State?
hint: This provision is invoked when constitutional machinery fails in a state.
summary: Article 356 empowers the President to impose President's Rule in a state.

title: 82. Which case established that Parliament cannot alter the Basic Structure of the Constitution?
hint: The judgment was delivered by the largest constitutional bench in Indian history.
summary: The Kesavananda Bharati case established the Basic Structure Doctrine.

title: 83. Which Article deals with the amendment of the Constitution?
hint: This Article lays down the procedure for constitutional amendments.
summary: Article 368 specifies the procedure for amending the Constitution.

title: 84. Which constitutional body conducts elections to Parliament and State Legislatures?
hint: It is established under Article 324.
summary: The Election Commission of India supervises and conducts elections.

title: 85. Which writ commands a public authority to perform its legal duty?
hint: Its Latin meaning is 'we command'.
summary: Mandamus is issued to direct a public authority to perform its duty.

title: 86. Which amendment added Part IX to the Constitution?
hint: It is associated with democratic decentralization in rural India.
summary: The 73rd Constitutional Amendment added Part IX relating to Panchayats.

title: 87. Which Schedule contains the list of recognized languages in India?
hint: The number of languages in this schedule increased through amendments.
summary: The Eighth Schedule contains the recognized languages of India.

title: 88. Which Article empowers the Supreme Court to issue writs for enforcement of Fundamental Rights?
hint: Dr. Ambedkar called this Article the heart and soul of the Constitution.
summary: Article 32 empowers the Supreme Court to issue writs.

title: 89. Which case is associated with the doctrine of judicial review being part of the basic structure?
hint: The judgment struck down parts of the 42nd Amendment.
summary: The Minerva Mills case reinforced judicial review as part of the basic structure.

title: 90. Which body recommends the distribution of tax revenues between the Centre and States?
hint: It is constituted every five years by the President.
summary: The Finance Commission recommends revenue-sharing arrangements.

title: 91. Which Article grants special status provisions for Jammu and Kashmir before its abrogation in 2019?
hint: It was located in Part XXI of the Constitution.
summary: Article 370 provided special constitutional provisions to Jammu and Kashmir.

title: 92. Which case is known for expanding the scope of Article 21?
hint: It involved the issue of a passport being impounded.
summary: The Maneka Gandhi case broadened the interpretation of the Right to Life and Personal Liberty.

title: 93. Which constitutional body is responsible for recruiting civil servants for the Union Government?
hint: Its name includes the word 'Public'.
summary: The Union Public Service Commission conducts recruitment for central services.

title: 94. Which Schedule was created to protect certain laws from judicial review?
hint: It was introduced by the First Constitutional Amendment.
summary: The Ninth Schedule was created to shield specific laws from judicial scrutiny.

title: 95. Which case emphasized that secularism is a basic feature of the Constitution?
hint: The case dealt extensively with Article 356 and federalism.
summary: The S.R. Bommai case recognized secularism as a basic feature.

title: 96. Which Article provides for the establishment of the Inter-State Council?
hint: It promotes coordination between the Union and States.
summary: Article 263 provides for the Inter-State Council.

title: 97. Which constitutional amendment is known as the Mini Constitution?
hint: It made extensive changes during the Emergency period.
summary: The 42nd Constitutional Amendment is known as the Mini Constitution.

title: 98. Which Article empowers High Courts to issue writs?
hint: Unlike the Supreme Court, this power extends beyond Fundamental Rights.
summary: Article 226 empowers High Courts to issue writs.

title: 99. Which committee recommended the establishment of Panchayati Raj Institutions in India?
hint: Its report was submitted in 1957.
summary: The Balwant Rai Mehta Committee recommended the Panchayati Raj system.

title: 100. Which constitutional office is known as the guardian of the Public Purse?
hint: This authority audits government expenditure after it has occurred.
summary: The Comptroller and Auditor General (CAG) safeguards public finances through auditing.
